Understanding Container Orchestration: A Visual Guide

Container orchestration manages the lifecycle of containers across a cluster. Here's how the key concepts fit together.

Scheduling

The scheduler decides which node should run each pod. It considers resource requirements, affinity rules, and current cluster utilization.

Service Discovery

Services provide a stable DNS name and IP address for a set of pods. This decouples service consumers from the specific pods that implement the service.

Self-Healing

When a pod crashes or a node goes down, the orchestrator automatically reschedules affected workloads. This self-healing behavior is one of the key benefits of container orchestration.

Understanding these concepts is the foundation for designing resilient, scalable containerized applications.
